name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's terminal-first agentic coding tool; reference CLI agent

developer.comment: Built by Anthropic, the maker of the Claude models.

first-release.comment: Released as a research preview in Feb 2025; reached general availability (v1.0.0) in May 2025 alongside Claude 4. Using the first public release per the guideline.

status.comment: Actively developed and GA; ships frequent updates across terminal, IDE, desktop, and web. Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: The Claude Code agent itself is proprietary/closed source; only the separate Agent SDK is published. License of the agent, not the model.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent for building features, fixing bugs, tests, refactors; not optimized for a single niche.

interface.comment: Ships as a terminal CLI, a VS Code and JetBrains extension, a standalone desktop app, and a browser/web version (claude.ai/code).

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal, as VS Code/Cursor and JetBrains plugins, and in the browser at claude.ai/code; also a standalone desktop app.

open-source.comment: The Claude Code agent is proprietary. The Agent SDK is separately available but the agent's own source is not openly licensed.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Primary shape is a CLI tool; also ships as an IDE extension and runs as a hosted cloud/web service for long-running tasks.

model-choice.comment: User can pick among Claude model tiers (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) but cannot switch to other providers' models from within the agent; only Claude models are supported.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supports Anthropic Console API key (pay-as-you-go) and own cloud accounts via Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry.

local-model-support.comment: No documented support for local/self-hosted models (Ollama, vLLM, etc.); third-party options are cloud providers serving Claude, plus optional LLM-gateway proxying of Anthropic traffic.

multi-file-editing.comment: Plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files in a single task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains edit -> run -> fix loops end-to-end (e.g. 'write tests, run them, and fix any failures') with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands, builds, and tests as part of its loop via a Bash tool.

mcp-support.comment: Native Model Context Protocol support for external tools/data sources (Jira, Slack, Google Drive, custom servers).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Spawns multiple agents/subagents that work in parallel with a lead agent coordinating; also background agents for parallel full sessions.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand via agentic search rather than building a persistent semantic/embeddings index of the repo.

planning-mode.comment: Has an explicit plan-then-execute flow; plan review is surfaced in the VS Code extension and the CLI plan mode.

skills.comment: Supports packaged, on-demand Skills and custom slash commands (e.g. /review-pr) that the team can share.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input such as screenshots and mockups, leveraging the underlying Claude models' vision capability.

browser-computer-use.comment: Chrome integration lets it drive and debug live web applications, beyond plain URL fetching.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational edits, supervised multi-step runs with per-action approval, autonomous task completion, and async/background runs via web and background agents. No tab inline-completion.

inline-completion.comment: Chat/agent-driven; does not provide as-you-type tab autocomplete suggestions.

background-async-execution.comment: Long-running tasks can run on the web/cloud and as background agents while the developer does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: CLAUDE.md project memory plus auto memory that saves learnings (build commands, debugging insights) across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Built-in /rewind checkpoints (since v2.0.0) snapshot edited files automatically and restore code/conversation/both without git.

git-integration.comment: Works directly with git: stages changes, writes commit messages, creates branches.

pr-creation.comment: Opens pull requests directly and via GitHub Actions / GitLab CI integrations.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Reads CLAUDE.md instruction files at project, repo, and org levels at the start of every session.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Hooks run shell commands before/after actions; plus custom skills/commands and the Agent SDK for programmatic extension.

permission-model.comment: Asks approval per action by default, is configurable (managed permissions, allow/deny rules), and offers a sandboxing mode (introduced Oct 2025).

self-host-on-prem.comment: Cannot be deployed fully on-prem; the agent calls Anthropic-hosted or cloud-provider (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) model endpoints. Corporate proxy/LLM-gateway routing is supported but the models remain remote.

privacy-mode.comment: Commercial API, Team, and Enterprise tiers do not use inputs/outputs to train models by default; consumer (Free/Pro/Max) usage offers an opt-out toggle. Checked 2026-06-02.

enterprise-controls.comment: Claude for Enterprise adds SSO/domain capture, role-based permissions, and compliance/audit (compliance API) controls; managed policy settings org-wide.

pricing-model.comment: Included with Pro/Max subscriptions; also usable pay-as-you-go via an Anthropic Console API account. No genuine free tier. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: No free tier; requires a paid Pro/Max subscription or pay-as-you-go API billing.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan including Claude Code is Pro at $20/mo (US), ~$17/mo if billed annually. Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since May 2025, widely deployed and polished across terminal/IDE/desktop/web; reference CLI agent with very large adoption.

community-size.comment: Per the guide's thresholds, Claude Code sits at the 'large' tier; widely adopted with a very active developer community.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Meaningful ecosystem of MCP servers, shareable skills/commands, hooks, and the Agent SDK; integrations across GitHub, GitLab, Slack, Chrome.
